title: Salesforce import truncates phone country codes to 2 digits since v2.3.1
problem: The nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow import is truncating international phone number country codes to exactly 2 digits. Country codes with 3+ digits are corrupted (e.g., +353 → +35, +1268 → +12), while 1-digit codes like +1 are unaffected. Approximately 50-100 of ~2,000 customer records are affected, causing support team members to reach wrong numbers.
root_cause_hypothesis: The TaskFlow v2.3.1 update likely introduced a change to the phone number parsing or normalization logic in the Salesforce import pipeline that hard-codes or caps the country code field at 2 digits. This could be a regex pattern like `\+(\d{1,2})` instead of `\+(\d{1,3})`, a substring operation like `countryCode.slice(0, 2)`, or a database column width constraint added during migration. The fact that +1 numbers are unaffected confirms the truncation ceiling is at 2 characters.
reproduction_steps: Set up a Salesforce contact record with an international phone number using a 3+ digit country code (e.g., +353 1 234 5678 for Ireland)
Run the built-in TaskFlow Salesforce import (or wait for the nightly sync)
Check the imported phone number in TaskFlow — expect the country code to be truncated to 2 digits (+35 1 234 5678)
Compare with a +1 (US/Canada) number imported in the same batch — expect it to be unaffected
environment: TaskFlow v2.3.1, built-in Salesforce integration configured via admin panel, nightly batch import schedule. Import configuration and Salesforce field mappings have not changed in months.
impact: ~50-100 customer contact records have corrupted phone numbers, preventing the support team from reaching customers. Ongoing — every nightly import re-corrupts any manually corrected numbers. Affects all customers with 3+ digit country codes (most non-NANP international numbers).
recommended_fix: 1. Review the v2.3.1 changelog and diff for changes to phone number parsing, formatting, or the Salesforce import pipeline. 2. Look for a hard-coded country code length limit (regex, substring, or DB column width) and extend it to support 1-3 digit country codes per E.164. 3. After fixing the parsing logic, run a one-time data repair: re-import affected records from Salesforce or write a migration script to restore truncated country codes. 4. Consider adding a validation check that flags phone numbers whose country code doesn't match any valid ITU-T code.
proposed_test_case: Unit test the phone number import/normalization function with country codes of varying lengths: +1 (1 digit, US), +44 (2 digits, UK), +353 (3 digits, Ireland), +1268 (4-digit NANP, Antigua). Assert that the full country code is preserved in all cases. Additionally, add an integration test that imports a Salesforce record with a +353 number and verifies the stored value matches exactly.
information_gaps: Exact v2.3.1 release notes and code diff — confirms root cause but requires dev team access
Whether the truncation occurs during import processing or on database write (column width constraint vs. parsing logic)
Whether existing corrupted records are being re-corrupted on each nightly run, preventing manual fixes from persisting
